name: ml-research
label: ML Research
description: Machine learning or deep learning research. Python-heavy, GPU compute, experiment tracking.
profile_description: GPU compute required for training runs. Use hosted or cluster inference for production.
run_context_additions: - Record for every run: model id, device, backend, batch size, runtime, artifact outputs.
- Cost acknowledgment required before any paid inference or training run.
- GPU memory is the primary constraint — log batch size and gradient accumulation settings.
- Prefer remote GPU or cluster for training; local machine for exploration and debugging only.
- On a cluster that uses environment modules rather than conda, `module load` the desired Python and set `EPISTEME_PYTHON_PREFIX` (or `EPISTEME_PYTHON`) to pin it.
workflow_notes: Use the `bounded-loop-runner` skill for hyperparameter sweeps and training loops.
Log experiment results to `docs/PROGRESS.md` after each significant run.
Record GPU backend, model id, and quota constraints in `docs/RUN_CONTEXT.md`.
Keep raw data, model weights, and checkpoints out of Git — add them to `.gitignore`.
Prefer small smoke-test runs locally before scaling to full compute.
safety_notes: Cost acknowledgment required before any paid inference or training run.
Never commit model weights, large datasets, or API keys.
Protect checkpoints from accidental deletion — verify paths before any `rm`.
Treat GPU quota as a shared resource — record usage in action_log.
